reason: Response A exhibits Haiku's characteristic tendency toward warm, accumulative example-listing (coffee, lunch-box notes, held doors, lent umbrellas) and earnest emotional summation ("I see you, I was thinking of you, you matter"), which matches Haiku's eager, slightly sentimental and list-heavy register. Response B's spare, essayistic argumentation with deliberate rhetorical restraint ("There is no need to sentimentalize this") and cost-benefit framing ("their ratio is very good") reads as a more analytical, restrained voice imitating a literary minimalism rather than Haiku's natural fullness. The genuine Haiku tends to over-deliver examples and lean into affirming feeling rather than the cooler, controlled prose of B.
